wordplay_note: Nominative irony — she is foolish and vulnerable
card_description: Susie Smart (meaning "Smart = ironic (she is not)") is a character in Philip K. Dick's *A Maze of Death*. Colonist whose intellectual pretensions contrast with her helplessness. Name note: Nominative irony — she is foolish and vulnerable. The gap between self-image and reality. First appears in *A Maze of Death*.

reference.brief: Colonist whose intellectual pretensions contrast with her helplessness
reference.significance: The gap between self-image and reality
